title: A Cross-platform Errand Service Application for Campus with a Dual-user Identity Model
authors: Chenliang Tian, Xinyi Fang, Xu Yang, Yapeng Wang
publication: IEEE ICSESS 2022 (International Conference on Software Engineering and Service Sciences)
key_findings: Campus errand service platform using dual-user identity model, blockchain for data backup, credit scoring system. Tested with 53 volunteers. Improved resource utilization and increased efficiency of student study and life.
relevance_to_viecz: Direct academic validation of campus errand service model. Demonstrates technical implementation using blockchain and dual-user architecture. Shows quantifiable benefits from student trials.

title: Connect Sphere: A Cost-Free, Peer-to-Peer Student Marketplace
publication: International Journal of Multidisciplinary Research and Free Research (IJFMR)
key_findings: Hyperlocal peer-to-peer marketplace addressing market thickness and trust deficit in student populations. Zero-commission model, mandatory .edu verification, hyperlocal search, real-time chat, reputation system.
relevance_to_viecz: Very recent (2025) academic work directly addressing campus marketplace challenges. Proposes solutions to trust and market thickness problems. Technology stack provided (React, Node.js, MongoDB).

title: Connect Sphere: A Hyper-Local Real-Time Digital Marketplace Architecture for University Communities
publication: International Journal on Advanced Computer Engineering and Communication Technology (IJACECT)
key_findings: System architecture for peer-to-peer university marketplace. React.js frontend, Node.js/Express backend, MongoDB database. Secure transactions, user reputation system.
relevance_to_viecz: Technical architecture reference for campus marketplace. System design patterns applicable to similar platforms.

title: Student Online Marketplace for University Community
key_findings: Addresses peer-to-peer commerce among university students. Focuses on building trust and market thickness within student communities.
relevance_to_viecz: Earlier research validating student marketplace concept. Identifies core challenges (trust, market thickness).

title: Smart Mobility in a Secondary City: Insights from Food Delivery App Adoption Among Thai University Students
key_findings: Study of 396 students at Mahasarakham University (Feb-June 2024). Analyzes adoption factors of delivery apps by Thai university students. Penetration rate of online food delivery in Thailand: 27.14% (Feb 2024).
relevance_to_viecz: Southeast Asian context. University student demographic. Delivery/logistics adoption patterns. Shows demand for on-demand services among Thai students.

title: Navigating the Gig Economy: What Drives Malaysian Youth?
publication: Journal of Applied Youth Studies (Springer Nature)
key_findings: Survey of 385 youth gig workers in Kuala Lumpur (Oct 2023 - Mar 2024). Majority are males aged 19-30 with tertiary education. Average monthly income RM 2001-4000. Key motivators: flexible hours, autonomy, additional income.
relevance_to_viecz: Southeast Asian gig economy research. Student and youth participation in gig work. Motivations align with campus platform value proposition.

title: On-Demand App Gig Work for Youth: A Case in Malaysia
key_findings: Analysis of youth participation in on-demand app-based gig work. Malaysia gig economy valued at RM 1.61 billion, 1.12M workers, 23% yearly growth rate.
relevance_to_viecz: Direct Southeast Asian research. Youth and student participation. Market size and growth metrics.

title: Socio-technical Issues in the Platform-mediated Gig Economy: A Systematic Literature Review
publication: Journal of the Association for Information Science and Technology (ARIST), Wiley Online Library
key_findings: Comprehensive systematic literature review. Identified 12 key themes on how digital platforms influence gig workers. Platforms offer opportunities (low barriers, flexible work) but create challenges (lack of social protections, algorithmic control, wage pressure).
relevance_to_viecz: Broader gig economy context. Platform design implications. Worker impact analysis.

title: Student-Centric E-Commerce Platform for Local Business Growth
publication: American Scientific Research Journal for Engineering, Technology, and Sciences
key_findings: Addresses how student-centric platforms can support local business growth. Explores student marketplace design.
relevance_to_viecz: Recent 2024 publication on student platform design. Economic implications for student communities.

title: An Effective Approach of Hyperlocal Based Services in Smart Cities
key_findings: Explores hyperlocal service delivery models in urban contexts. Relevant to campus-as-smart-city paradigm.
relevance_to_viecz: Hyperlocal delivery architecture and principles. Urban deployment patterns.

title: Online Marketplace: Student Consumer Strategies
key_findings: Examines how students use online marketplaces. Consumer behavior and decision-making patterns.
relevance_to_viecz: Student consumer behavior on platforms. Market research relevance.

title: Gig Economy Insights: Key Trends, Influencers and Research Directions
publication: Global Knowledge, Memory and Communication (Emerald Publishing)
key_findings: Analysis of gig economy trends, key influencers, research directions. Market growth and platform evolution.
relevance_to_viecz: Broader market trends. Platform dynamics and evolution.

title: Analysing the Evolution and Patterns of the Gig Economy: A Bibliometric Examination of Growth and Trends
key_findings: Bibliometric analysis showing highest publications in 2023 (126 documents). Growth rate ~40.29% annually. Covers diverse gig platforms from data entry to food delivery.
relevance_to_viecz: Research trend analysis. Emerging research focus areas.

title: Transformative Dynamics of the Gig Economy: Technological Impacts, Worker Well-being and Global Research Trends
authors: Rachoru Himani Srihita, Gurunadham Goli, Rajyalaxmi M, R Gobinath
key_findings: Analysis of technological impacts on gig workers, well-being implications, global research trends through 2024-2025.
relevance_to_viecz: Worker impact analysis. Technology implications for gig platforms. Recent 2025 research.

title: The Gig Economy and the Evolving Nature of Work in India: Employment, Policy, and Platform Realities in the Age of Convenience
key_findings: Analysis of India's gig economy growth driven by technology and shifting employer preferences. Range of gig jobs from freelance work to ride-hailing. Regional relevance to South/Southeast Asia.
relevance_to_viecz: South Asian context. Gig economy maturity in region. Policy implications.

title: An Analysis of Online Transportation Applications Between Gojek and Grab for Students
key_findings: Comparative analysis of major Southeast Asian super-apps (Gojek, Grab) as used by students. Transportation and delivery features.
relevance_to_viecz: Regional competitor analysis. Student usage patterns of major platforms.
